free_text: Fission of lead induced by 600 MeV protons
Four Ge(Li) detectors. Most of the
results are determined with at least two detectors.
The detectors were coupled to different amplifier and
multichannel analyzer systems.
Cascade calculations.
correction in some subentries.
Total fission cross section is included.
Last checking has been done.
Date is corrected
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents.
The stack consisted of a target foil of Pb 99.999%
0.5 mm thick with catcher foils of 30 um aluminium,
monitor foils of 10 um Al and guard foils of 30 um
aluminium on both sides.
Rb was separated from the target solution
by a perchlorate precipitation and further purified by
series of hydroxide-,sulfide- and carbonate-scavenging
to remove contamination.
Number of determinations.
The errors are standard deviations based
on errors in gamma-peak area analysis, counting
efficiencies and counting statistics, chemical yield
measurements monitor cross section, and spread among
the parallel determinations. Errors in the half-lives
and absolute gamma-ray intensities are not included.
Tbl.2 from J.Inorg.Nucl.Chem.,37(1975)1569
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.

The stack consisted of a target foil of Pb 99.999%
0.5 mm thick with catcher foils of 30 um aluminium,
monitor foils of 10 um Al and guard foils of 30 um
aluminium on both sides.
Ag was separated using a procedure,
consisting of a series of AgCl and Ag(2)S
precipitations and Fe(OH)(3)-scavenging.
The final sample is AgCl.
Number of determinations.
The errors are standard deviations based
on errors in gamma-peak area analysis, counting
efficiencies and counting statistics, chemical yield
measurements monitor cross section, and spread among
the parallel determinations. Errors in the half-lives
and absolute gamma-ray intensities are not included.

The stack consisted of a target foil of Pb 99.999%
0.5 mm thick with catcher foils of 30 um aluminium,
monitor foils of 10 um Al and guard foils of 30 um
aluminium on both sides.
Cd was separated using a procedure-several
precipitations of CdS at pH approx.1.5 and scavengers
with PdS and Sb(2)S(3) from 2N HCl are performed. The
solution of Cd is placed on a Dowex 2 anion exchange
column which is then washed through with 0.1N HCl
without eluting Cd. Cd is then eluted with 1.5m
H(2)SO(4). The final sample is CdS.
Number of determinations.
The errors are standard deviations based
on errors in gamma-peak area analysis, counting
efficiencies and counting statistics, chemical yield
measurements monitor cross section, and spread among
the parallel determinations. Errors in the half-lives
and absolute gamma-ray intensities are not included.
Tbl.2 from J.Inorg.Nucl.Chem.,37(1975)1569
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated. ERR-T -> DATA-ERR.
- by compiler - authors have beleived that Cd-115-m
production is independent. But Cd-115-m could be
produced due to ground state Ag-115 decay.
The stack consisted of a target foil of Pb 99.999%
0.5 mm thick with catcher foils of 30 um aluminium,
monitor foils of 10 um Al and guard foils of 30 um
aluminium on both sides.
Cd was separated using a procedure-several
precipitations of cds at ph approx.1.5 and scavengers
with PdS and Sb(2)S(3) from 2N HCl are performed. The
solution of Cd is placed on a Dowex 2 anion exchange
column which is then washed through with 0.1N HCl
without eluting Cd. Cd is then eluted with 1.5m
H(2)SO(4). The final sample is CdS.
Number of determinations.
The errors are standard deviations based
on errors in gamma-peak area analysis, counting
efficiencies and counting statistics, chemical yield
measurements monitor cross section, and spread among
the parallel determinations. Errors in the half-lives
and absolute gamma-ray intensities are not included.
